done_summary:
Triaged all 30 support tickets from `/input/repo/tickets.txt`. Produced:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with:
- All 30 tickets classified into 12 categories (bug-report: 7, billing-refund: 4, feature-request: 3, incident-active: 2, spam-or-noise: 4, account-management: 2, auth-permissions: 2, external-business: 2, general-question: 2, legal-compliance: 1, security-incident: 1, billing-other: 0)
- Urgency levels: 6 urgent, 14 normal, 6 low, 4 n/a
- 2 duplicate clusters identified: [007, 011] (dashboard sharing permissions) and [014, 023, 030] (API rate limits)
- Detailed notes explaining non-obvious classification decisions

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Human-readable summary with urgency-prioritized tables, duplicate cluster explanations, methodology, and recommended action order.

**`/workspace/decisions/adr-001-through-005.md`** — Architecture Decision Records for 5 non-obvious calls:
- ADR-001: Ticket 014 (rate limits as bug-report vs billing-other)
- ADR-002: Ticket 029 (extortion as spam vs security-incident)
- ADR-003: Tickets 007/011 (duplicate detection)
- ADR-004: Ticket 016 (500 errors as incident-active vs bug-report)
- ADR-005: Ticket 022 (downtime credit as billing-refund vs incident-active)

Top urgent items for immediate action: 028 (security compromise), 003 (production outage), 016 (API 500 errors), 014/023/030 (rate limit blocking $48k customer).
